历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plans
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum rankings for science/physics category were approximately 23903 → 28948 → 45296 → 39717 → 34167. A significant backward shift occurred in 2023, followed by two consecutive years of recovery in 2024–2025, with a cumulative forward shift of about 11129 positions over the past two years. The baseline for 2025 is approximately 34167. The university's enrollment plan in Guizhou for science/physics category was 59 students in both 2021 and 2022, 61 in 2023, 56 in 2024, and 62 in 2025. In 2026, the plan significantly increases to 106 students, an expansion of about 71% compared to 2025, greatly alleviating competition pressure.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: A 211 Project and Double First-Class construction university. It ranks approximately 115th on the Soft Science main list (2026), with stable rankings in the 113–115 range in recent years. Its reputation has been stable recently, with no significant negative public opinion. On Douyin (TikTok), its popularity rating is 'relatively hot' (ranked 59th overall), indicating moderate-to-high attention on short video platforms. No obvious records of admission cut-off gaps have been observed.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: Engineering majors such as Computer Science, Electronic Information/Communication, and Electrical Engineering continue to be socially popular, matching the university's enrollment majors. In 2026, the number of physics category examinees in Guizhou is approximately 208,000, a slight increase of about 0.6% from 2025 (207,000), leading to slightly intensified competition. The university is located in Harbin, Heilongjiang Province, far from Guizhou, making it a cross-provincial enrollment. From 2021 to 2025, the university's enrollment proportion in Guizhou was about 3.8%–4.0%, remaining stable.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the 2025 baseline ranking of 34167, the significant enrollment expansion of about 71% in 2026 (greatly alleviating competition), a slight increase of about 0.6% in physics category examinees (slightly intensifying competition), and moderate-to-high Douyin popularity, the minimum admission ranking in 2026 is expected to shift backward significantly (numerical increase). However, the large expansion may attract more applicants, so the actual backward shift will be slightly smaller than the pure expansion effect. The predicted minimum ranking is approximately 48515, with a range of 48015–49015.

major_tier_recommendation_en.overall_advice: In 2026, the physics category enrollment plan significantly expands to 106 students, and the ranking is expected to shift backward notably. For rankings within the predicted range (48015–49015), it is recommended to focus on Tier 2 majors, with Tier 1 as a reach and Tier 3 as a safety. For rankings significantly better than the lower bound (< 48015), prioritize competitive majors like Computer Science and Electronic Information. For rankings near the upper bound (> 49015), focus mainly on Tier 3 majors to secure admission.
